items.briefing:
A recent study found that most organisations have already experienced AI-related security incidents, highlighting growing risks as AI tools become more common in the workplace. Separately, research suggests that AI is helping cyber attackers more than defenders, meaning threats may become faster and harder to detect. On a more positive note, Australian individuals face a reduced overall cybercrime risk compared to previous years — however, pressure is shifting toward small and medium businesses (SMBs), which remain attractive targets due to typically weaker defences.

If your organisation uses AI tools, take a moment to review what data staff are sharing with them — avoid entering sensitive client or business information into AI platforms unless they've been formally approved for that purpose.

featured.title: Most firms hit by AI security incidents, study finds
featured.quote: Most Australian organisations are exposed to AI security breaches, requiring urgent defensive action.
